pattern: Pigeonhole bucketing by a uniform interval guarantees the maximum gap spans bucket boundaries, so only adjacent buckets' extremes need comparing for linear time.
hints: Sorting would solve it, but the linear-time requirement hints that you can avoid comparing every pair.
If you spread the values into buckets of equal width, the biggest gap must occur between one bucket's maximum and the next non-empty bucket's minimum, never inside a bucket.
Choose bucket width about (max-min)/(n-1), drop each value into its bucket keeping that bucket's min and max, then scan buckets in order comparing the previous max to the current min.

edge_inputs.description: two elements, gap is their difference

stress.description: large array of random values across the full range
stress.note: wide spread to create varied gaps
